Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Patients and Methods' section, it states: 'In this phase III study, 790 patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were equally randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles or ADT alone.' This is further confirmed on page 3 in the 'Results' section under 'Patients': 'Between July 2006 and December 2012, 790 eligible patients were enrolled (Fig 1), including 397 in the ADT plus docetaxel arm'. Figure 1 also shows 397 patients assigned to ADT plus docetaxel and 393 patients assigned to ADT alone, totaling 790 participants.

Control Arm.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Patients and Methods' section, it states: '790 patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were equally randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles or ADT alone.' The 'ADT alone' arm serves as the comparison group, not receiving the experimental docetaxel treatment. This is further supported by Figure 1 on page 2, which labels one arm as 'Randomly assigned to ADT alone (n = 393)'.

Treatment Arm(s).value: ADT in combination with docetaxel; ADT alone
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 states: 'In this phase III study, 790 patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were equally randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles or ADT alone.' Figure 1 and Table 1 also clearly label these two arms as 'ADT plus docetaxel' and 'ADT alone'.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 states: 'In this phase III study, 790 patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were equally randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles or ADT alone.' Treatment Arm 1 is the experimental arm, which is ADT in combination with docetaxel.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 states: "In this phase III study, 790 patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were equally randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles or ADT alone." This indicates two treatment arms. The CONSORT diagram (Fig 1) on page 2 also shows two arms: 'Randomly assigned to ADT plus docetaxel' and 'Randomly assigned to ADT alone'.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 2, under 'PATIENTS AND METHODS' and 'Study Design and Participants', it states: 'Between July 2006 and December 2012, 790 eligible patients were enrolled (Fig 1), including 397 in the ADT plus docetaxel arm'. Figure 1, 'CONSORT diagram', also explicitly shows 'Randomly assigned to ADT plus docetaxel (n = 397)'. This number is consistently reported in Table 1, Table 2, and Table 3 under 'ADT Plus Docetaxel' and 'No. of Patients' for 'Overall'.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: On page 1, in the 'Results' section, it states: 'At a median follow-up of 53.7 months, the median OS was 57.6 months for the chemohormonal therapy arm versus 47.2 months for ADT alone'. On page 4, in the 'Efficacy' section, it also states: 'This report represents data with a cutoff date for survival of April 23, 2016, resulting in a median follow-up of 53.7 months'.

Control Arm - N.evidence: On page 2, in the 'PATIENTS AND METHODS' section under 'Study Design and Participants', it states: 'Between July 2006 and December 2012, 790 eligible patients were enrolled (Fig 1), including 397 in the ADT plus docetaxel arm and 393 in the ADT alone arm.' The 'ADT alone' arm is the control arm. This is further confirmed by Figure 1 (CONSORT diagram) on page 2, which shows 'Randomly assigned to ADT alone (n = 393)', and by Table 1, Table 2, and Table 3 on pages 3 and 6 respectively, where the 'ADT Alone' arm consistently lists '393' patients for 'Overall'.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The 'ABSTRACT' section states: '...patients...were equally randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m²...'. The paper's title is 'Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer', and the 'Results' section refers to 'chemohormonal therapy arm'. ADT is androgen-deprivation therapy (hormonal) and docetaxel is a cytotoxic chemotherapy agent.

Trial Name.value: Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer: Long-Term Survival Analysis of the Randomized Phase III E3805 CHAARTED Trial
Trial Name.evidence: Found as the main title of the article on page 1.

Author.evidence: Found listed as the authors under the title on page 1. Using 'et al.' for brevity.

Year.evidence: Found 'APRIL 10, 2018' and '©2018 by American Society of Clinical Oncology' on page 1.

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is a multi-page article from the 'JOURNAL OF CLINICAL ONCOLOGY' with detailed sections (Purpose, Patients and Methods, Results, Conclusion, Introduction, Discussion), indicating a full publication.

Phase.evidence: Found in the title 'Randomized Phase III E3805 CHAARTED Trial' and in the 'Patients and Methods' section 'In this phase III study' on page 1.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The title includes 'Long-Term Survival Analysis' and the abstract states 'Herein, we present the outcomes of the CHAARTED... trial with more mature follow-up' on page 1.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The 'Purpose' and 'Patients and Methods' sections of the abstract, as well as the 'Study Design and Participants' and 'Study Assessments' sections, detail the primary and secondary endpoints as overall survival (OS), time to development of CRPC, and time to clinical progression. While the introduction mentions 'improve quality of life and OS' as a general strategy for combining novel therapies (citing references 3-6), this specific paper does not report results on the quality of life of participants. The 'Results' and 'Discussion' sections also do not mention any findings related to quality of life.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The document reports 'Median OS (Overall Survival)' and 'Median Time to CRPC' and 'Median Time to Clinical Progression' in months for both treatment and control arms, but does not explicitly state the 'Median On-Treatment Duration (mo)' for the treatment arm.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The document reports 'Median OS (Overall Survival)' and 'Median Time to CRPC' and 'Median Time to Clinical Progression' in months for both treatment and control arms, but does not explicitly state the 'Median On-Treatment Duration (mo)' for the control arm.

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document mentions 'improve quality of life and OS' in the introduction as a general potential benefit of a strategy, but it does not report any specific measure, scale, questionnaire, or survey used to assess the participants' overall well-being, satisfaction with health status, or perceived quality of life during the clinical trial. The study's primary and secondary endpoints, as detailed in the 'Patients and Methods' and 'Study Assessments' sections, focus on overall survival, time to CRPC, and clinical progression.

Trial.value: CHAARTED (Chemohormonal Therapy Versus Androgen Ablation Randomized Trial for Extensive Disease in Prostate Cancer) trial
Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study is explicitly stated in the title and the 'Purpose' section on page 1: 'Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer: Long-Term Survival Analysis of the Randomized Phase III E3805 CHAARTED Trial' and 'CHAARTED (Chemohormonal Therapy Versus Androgen Ablation Randomized Trial for Extensive Disease in Prostate Cancer) trial'.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: On page 4, in the 'Efficacy' section, it states: 'The median age was 64 years (range, 36 to 88 years) in the combination arm versus 63 years (range, 39 to 91 years) in the ADT alone arm.' The 'combination arm' refers to the treatment arm.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: On page 4, in the 'Efficacy' section, it states: 'The median age was 64 years (range, 36 to 88 years) in the combination arm versus 63 years (range, 39 to 91 years) in the ADT alone arm.' The 'ADT alone arm' refers to the control arm.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Found in the 'Efficacy' section on page 4, first paragraph, stating '263 patients (66.2%) in the combination arm ... had high-volume disease.' The combination arm refers to the treatment arm.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Found in the 'Efficacy' section on page 4, first paragraph, stating '...and 250 patients (63.6%) in the ADT alone arm had high-volume disease.' The ADT alone arm refers to the control arm.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Derived from Table 1 on page 3, 'Low volume' row under 'ADT Alone' shows 143 patients. The total number of patients in the ADT alone arm is 393 (from the 'Overall' row, 'ADT Alone' column in Table 1). The percentage is calculated as (143 / 393) * 100 = 36.4%.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 states 'Additional analyses of the prospectively defined low- and high-volume disease subgroups were performed.' The 'Results' section on page 1 and 'Table 1. OS and No. of Deaths by Subgroup' on page 3, as well as 'Fig 3. Kaplan-Meier estimates of overall survival (OS)' on page 4, present data for 'High volume/de novo' and 'Low volume/de novo' patients. 'De novo metastatic disease' is synonymous with synchronous metastases (metastases present at diagnosis), as further clarified on page 4: '289 patients (72.8%) in the combination arm versus 286 patients (72.8%) in the ADT alone arm had had no PLT and were considered to have de novo metastatic disease.'

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The 'Table 1. OS and No. of Deaths by Subgroup' on page 3 and 'Fig 3. Kaplan-Meier estimates of overall survival (OS)' on page 4 present data for 'High volume/prior local therapy' and 'Low volume/prior local therapy'. 'Prior local therapy (PLT)' implies that metastases occurred after initial treatment of the primary tumor, which aligns with the definition of metachronous disease. The text on page 5 also discusses 'patients with high-volume disease with PLT' and 'low-volume subgroups... with PLT'.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 explicitly states: 'Additional analyses of the prospectively defined low- and high-volume disease subgroups were performed. High-volume disease was defined as presence of visceral metastases and/or ≥ four bone metastases with at least one outside of the vertebral column and pelvis.' The 'Results' and 'Conclusion' sections on page 1, 'Table 1. OS and No. of Deaths by Subgroup' on page 3, and 'Fig 3. Kaplan-Meier estimates of overall survival (OS)' on page 4 all report outcomes specifically for 'High volume' patients.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 explicitly states: 'Additional analyses of the prospectively defined low- and high-volume disease subgroups were performed.' The 'Results' and 'Conclusion' sections on page 1, 'Table 1. OS and No. of Deaths by Subgroup' on page 3, and 'Fig 3. Kaplan-Meier estimates of overall survival (OS)' on page 4 all report outcomes specifically for 'Low volume' patients.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: The 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 states that 790 patients were equally randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel or ADT alone. The CONSORT diagram (Fig 1) on page 2 shows 'Randomly assigned to ADT plus docetaxel (n = 397)'. Below this, it indicates 'Included in Toxicity analysis (n = 390)'. The difference of 7 patients is accounted for in 'Did not start treatment (excluded from toxicity analysis)' due to withdrawal, medical decision, or no information. Therefore, 390 out of 397 participants in the treatment arm received docetaxel, which is (390/397)*100 = 98.2%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: The 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 states that 790 patients were equally randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel or ADT alone. The CONSORT diagram (Fig 1) on page 2 shows 'Randomly assigned to ADT alone (n = 393)'. This arm was designed to receive ADT alone, without docetaxel. There is no indication in the document that participants in the control arm received docetaxel before or during the trial as part of their assigned treatment. Therefore, 0 participants in the control arm received docetaxel.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: On page 4, under 'Efficacy', it states: '289 patients (72.8%) in the combination arm... had had no PLT and were considered to have de novo metastatic disease.' 'De novo metastatic disease' is interpreted as synchronous metastases. The combination arm is the treatment arm.

On page 4, under 'Efficacy', it states: '...versus 286 patients (72.8%) in the ADT alone arm had had no PLT and were considered to have de novo metastatic disease.' 'De novo metastatic disease' is interpreted as synchronous metastases. The ADT alone arm is the control arm.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: On page 3, under 'Patients', it states 397 eligible patients were enrolled in the ADT plus docetaxel arm (treatment arm). On page 4, it states 289 patients (72.8%) in the combination arm had 'de novo metastatic disease' (synchronous). Therefore, the number of patients with metachronous metastases (those with prior local therapy, PLT) in the treatment arm is 397 - 289 = 108. The percentage is (108/397) * 100 = 27.2%.

On page 3, under 'Patients', it states 393 eligible patients were enrolled in the ADT alone arm (control arm). On page 4, it states 286 patients (72.8%) in the ADT alone arm had 'de novo metastatic disease' (synchronous). Therefore, the number of patients with metachronous metastases (those with prior local therapy, PLT) in the control arm is 393 - 286 = 107. The percentage is (107/393) * 100 = 27.2%.

Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 1, in the 'Patients and Methods' section, it states: 'The primary end point of the study was overall survival (OS)'. This is further confirmed on page 3, in the 'Study Assessments' section, which states: 'The primary end point of the study is OS, defined as time from random assignment until death resulting from any cause.'

TTPSA (mo) | Treatment.evidence: The value for 'TTPSA (mo) | Treatment' is found in Table 2, titled 'Time to CRPC (PSA rise or clinical progression)'. For the 'Overall' subgroup under 'ADT Plus Docetaxel' (treatment arm), the 'Median Time (months)' is 19.4.

TTPSA (mo) | Control.evidence: The value for 'TTPSA (mo) | Control' is found in Table 2, titled 'Time to CRPC (PSA rise or clinical progression)'. For the 'Overall' subgroup under 'ADT Alone' (control arm), the 'Median Time (months)' is 11.7.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document is a research paper detailing the long-term survival analysis of a specific randomized controlled trial (CHAARTED trial). It presents results, statistical analyses (e.g., hazard ratios, p-values), and discusses the findings and limitations of the trial. However, it does not provide an 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE)' in randomized controlled trials (RCTs) in a general sense, nor does it use the specific terminology 'COE' or 'overall risk judgment' for individual criteria of evidence.

Type of Therapy.value: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) in combination with docetaxel or ADT alone
Type of Therapy.evidence: The 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 states: 'In this phase III study, 790 patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were equally randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles or ADT alone.'

Treatment Class.evidence: The title of the paper is 'Chemohormonal Therapy in Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer'. The 'Patients and Methods' section on page 1 states that participants were 'randomly assigned to receive either ADT in combination with docetaxel 75 mg/m² for up to six cycles or ADT alone'. The 'Results' section on page 1 also refers to 'chemohormonal therapy arm' and 'ADT alone'.
